description_long: This is a large, heavy-duty sledgehammer with a robust, rectangular double-faced head made of dark, weathered metal. The head features prominent bolts or rivets on its sides and a textured, aged surface, suggesting extensive use or exposure to the elements. The handle is long, cylindrical, and appears to be reinforced with metal, showing a rough, utilitarian finish with some visible wear and grime. The grip section of the handle is slightly textured, likely for improved handling, and the overall construction is solid and industrial. The hammer's proportions and rugged design indicate it is intended for heavy striking tasks, such as breaking concrete or demolition work in urban environments. There are no visible brand markings, but the tool exudes a sense of durability and strength.
description: A large, weathered sledgehammer with a double-faced metal head and a long, reinforced handle, showing signs of heavy use and rugged construction.
description_view_0: Front view shows the broad, rectangular striking face of the hammer head and the cylindrical handle extending directly toward the viewer.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the thick, rectangular profile of the hammer head and the full length of the handle, revealing surface texture and wear.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the opposite striking face of the head, with visible bolts or rivets and the handle receding away.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the other side of the head and handle, with similar surface details and weathering.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight, cylindrical handle made of wood and reinforced with metal, featuring a rough, textured grip area for secure handling.
attribute_head_type: Large, rectangular double-faced sledgehammer head made of dark, weathered metal with visible bolts or rivets and flat striking faces.
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: Visible signs of wear, including surface scratches, grime, and weathering on both the head and handle.
